creator: jon@xyinn.org
text:
Re-opening since the problem has re-occurred. Given that FreeBSD doesn't support sleep/resume on my laptop, the machine needs to be powered off. Once the machine is resumed, there is a chance that the clock will be off. I've retried all the steps mentioned here and also in these threads:

https://forums.freebsd.org/threads/the-system-clock-is-falling-behind.61450/
https://forums.freebsd.org/threads/ntpd-not-syncing-on-startup.68933/

I've also tried a combination of -g, -G, and -g -G with no success. I'm attaching my configs and output (as I said earlier, it's basically a vanilla FreeBSD 13.1 /etc/ntp.conf configuration with some recent tweaks to file paths for /var/db/ntp/* files. All the testing previously mentioned was done on the standard file paths for drift files, etc. Either way I've ensured these have the proper permissions and are being used.

I also tried setting the date to the exact minute on my phone's clock (which is correct) and even having a proper time didn't allow a connection. I've also tried the simple configuration recommended at: https://www.pool.ntp.org/en/use.html

and that didn't work either. Using specific servers rather than pools.

driftfile /var/lib/ntp/ntp.drift

server 0.pool.ntp.org
server 1.pool.ntp.org
server 2.pool.ntp.org
server 3.pool.ntp.org

(I also tried using iburst at the end of each option and nothing). At this point I'm out of ideas and I'll re-iterate that I don't see how ntpdate could be removed given that -g / -G are not working correctly.

---

Actual Time: 08:33 EDT

root@leslie:~ # date
Thu May 26 11:52:14 EDT 2022


---

root@leslie:~ # ls -l /var/db/ntp
total 10
-rw-r--r--  1 ntpd  ntpd      2 May 26 11:53 ntpd.drift
-rw-r--r--  1 ntpd  ntpd  10666 May 24 14:45 ntpd.leap-seconds.list
-rw-r--r--  1 ntpd  ntpd      5 May 26 11:50 ntpd.pid

---

Running the same command that would be run if I did `service ntpd start` excluding the -q flag since I want to see output in stdout and I don't want it to go into the background:

root@leslie:~ # ntpd -p /var/db/ntp/ntpd.pid -c /etc/ntp.conf -f /var/db/ntp/ntpd.drift -g -q
